key_highlights: Fed holds rates steady, inflation remains elevated due to Iran war, with dissents hinting at future rate cuts being delayed.
Iran geopolitical crisis deepens: Hormuz blockade, UAE exits OPEC, oil prices hit 4-year highs, then ease slightly.
Big Tech earnings mixed: Alphabet and Microsoft beat but AI spending surges to $190B, Meta plunges on raised capex forecast to $125-145B, tech stocks sell off.
Gas prices hit $4.30/gallon national average, a 4-year high, pressuring consumers and inflation.
BOE and ECB hold rates, with ECB debating a potential hike in June, signaling global tightening bias.
Treasury I bond rate set at 4.26%, US Treasuries rebound as oil eases from highs.
Mega-cap AI capex boom drives divergent market: Dow rises on industrials (Caterpillar), Nasdaq sinks on tech sell-off.
Geopolitical risks (Iran, Taiwan, China-US tensions) and energy supply disruptions dominate macro outlook.

sectors.trend: Divergent performance: Alphabet and Microsoft beat earnings but AI capex spending ramps to $190B (Microsoft) and $125-145B (Meta). Meta stock plunges, tech sell-off deepens. OpenAI reportedly missing key targets adds pressure. Semiconductor stocks rise on Qualcomm beat, but overall tech sentiment negative due to capex fears.
sectors.implications: AI spending boom is real but markets are punishing companies that raise capex without immediate revenue payoff. Expect continued volatility in mega-cap tech. Nvidia and Micron may benefit from Meta's capex plans, but broader tech may underperform.

sectors.trend: Oil prices surged to 4-year highs due to Iran war and Hormuz blockade, then eased slightly. UAE exits OPEC, adding supply uncertainty. Gas prices hit $4.30/gallon national average. Trump meets oil execs, Iran stalemate continues. Renewables vulnerability highlighted.
sectors.implications: Energy sector remains highly volatile with geopolitical risk premium. Oil prices likely to stay elevated, benefiting producers but hurting consumers and inflation-sensitive sectors. Renewables may see increased investment as countries seek energy independence.

sectors.trend: Fed holds rates steady, dissents suggest rate cuts in doubt. BOE holds but considers hikes. ECB holds, debates June hike. US Treasuries rebound as oil eases. Junk issuers in Europe switch to fixed rates. Berkshire's share price ails ahead of Abel's first meeting.
sectors.implications: Global central banks remain hawkish, with rate cuts unlikely in near term. Bond markets may stabilize if oil prices ease further. Financials may benefit from higher rates but face credit risk if economy slows.

sectors.trend: Yum Brands beats on Taco Bell strength. Gas prices at 4-year high pressure consumer spending. SoFi's unchanged forecast disappoints. Spirit Airlines struggles as big airlines compete. Uber adds hotel bookings, expanding travel platform.
sectors.implications: Consumer discretionary faces headwinds from energy costs. Fast food and value-oriented brands may outperform. Travel demand remains resilient but could soften if gas prices persist. E-commerce and travel platforms expanding.

sectors.trend: Caterpillar soars past entry, Textron leads defense advance. Data center infrastructure deals (Cardinal Infrastructure, AirTrunk, MARA). Elevator company mega-merger ($24B). Builders FirstSource weak but nearing bottom.
sectors.implications: Infrastructure and defense spending remain strong. Data center buildout continues to drive demand for industrial companies. Housing-related stocks may be bottoming. Defense sector benefits from geopolitical tensions.

sectors.trend: Eli Lilly has 'blowout' quarter with overwhelming demand. Oruka Therapeutics reports positive Phase 2a psoriasis results. Medpace and Align Technology see insider buying. Globus Medical attracts Bares Capital.
sectors.implications: Healthcare remains a defensive safe haven with strong earnings. Biotech has selective opportunities. Insider buying signals confidence. Eli Lilly's demand highlights GLP-1 drug class strength.

sectors.trend: DR Horton analyst report. Mortgage rate forecast for May 2026. Australian home prices slowest since Jan 2025. Builders FirstSource weak but nearing bottom.
sectors.implications: Housing market remains under pressure from high rates and inflation. Mortgage rates likely to stay elevated. Homebuilders may be near cyclical lows, but recovery depends on rate cuts.

sectors.trend: Iran war, Hormuz blockade, UAE exits OPEC. Taiwan's Cheng Li-wun seeks Trump meeting after Beijing trip. Chinese investment in US stagnant. RBI's short dollar book surges past $100B. SPEM emerging markets ETF has 3,000 holdings.
sectors.implications: Geopolitical risks dominate EM outlook. Energy-exporting EMs may benefit from higher oil prices, but importers face inflation. Taiwan tensions remain elevated. India's forex reserves strong. EM allocation may pay off for patient investors.

key_themes: Geopolitical risk premium: Iran war, Hormuz blockade, UAE OPEC exit, Taiwan tensions
AI capex boom vs. market punishment: Mega-cap tech spending surges, stocks sell off
Central bank hawkishness: Fed, BOE, ECB hold rates, rate cuts unlikely near term
Energy inflation: Gas prices at 4-year high, oil volatility, consumer pressure
Divergent market: Dow up on industrials, Nasdaq down on tech
Consumer resilience tested by energy costs
Emerging market opportunities amid geopolitical shifts
Mega-mergers and corporate restructuring (elevator, rare earth, data centers)

risk_assessment.mitigation: Increase exposure to energy producers, defense stocks, and commodities. Reduce exposure to consumer discretionary and airlines. Hedge with oil futures or energy ETFs.

risk_assessment.risk_factor: AI Capex Bubble / Tech Earnings Disappointment
risk_assessment.mitigation: Diversify away from mega-cap tech into value and defensive sectors. Focus on companies with clear AI revenue paths (e.g., Nvidia, Microsoft) vs. those with vague capex plans. Consider shorting overvalued AI hype stocks.
Allocate to TIPS, commodities, and real assets. Reduce duration in bond portfolios. Favor companies with pricing power (e.g., Eli Lilly, Caterpillar). Avoid high-growth, high-valuation stocks.

risk_assessment.risk_factor: Central Bank Policy Error (Hiking Too Late or Too Early)
risk_assessment.mitigation: Maintain balanced duration exposure. Favor floating-rate bonds. Monitor Fed, ECB, BOE forward guidance closely. Be prepared for volatility in rate-sensitive sectors.

risk_assessment.risk_factor: Consumer Spending Slowdown Due to Energy Costs
risk_assessment.mitigation: Reduce exposure to discretionary retail, airlines, and leisure. Increase exposure to value-oriented consumer staples (e.g., Yum Brands, Walmart). Monitor gas prices as leading indicator.
Maintain diversified global portfolio with EM exposure hedged. Increase allocation to US defense stocks and energy. Avoid concentrated exposure to China or Taiwan-dependent supply chains.
Reduce high-yield exposure. Favor investment-grade bonds. Monitor Air Baltic restructuring and European junk issuers switching to fixed rates as early warning signs.

opportunities.recommendation: Buy energy producers and oil services stocks on pullbacks
opportunities.rationale: Iran war and Hormuz blockade keep oil prices elevated. UAE exit from OPEC adds supply uncertainty. Energy sector benefits from sustained high prices. Look for companies with low production costs and strong balance sheets.

Data center buildout (MARA, Cardinal Infrastructure, AirTrunk) and defense spending (Textron, AeroVironment) are secular trends. Geopolitical tensions and AI demand drive long-term growth. Caterpillar's strong performance confirms industrial demand.

opportunities.recommendation: Buy Eli Lilly and other GLP-1 leaders on dips
opportunities.rationale: Eli Lilly's 'blowout' quarter with overwhelming demand shows GLP-1 drug class strength. Healthcare is defensive in current environment. Insider buying in Medpace and Align Technology signals confidence.

opportunities.recommendation: Consider emerging markets exposure via SPEM or similar ETFs
opportunities.rationale: Patient investors may benefit from EM allocation as geopolitical shifts create opportunities. India's RBI has strong forex reserves. China-US tensions may lead to supply chain diversification. Diversified EM ETF reduces single-country risk.
Inflation remains elevated due to energy costs. I bond rate at 4.26% is attractive. Treasuries rebounded as oil eased, but further volatility expected. Short-duration bonds reduce interest rate risk.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Reduce exposure to mega-cap tech (especially Meta, Microsoft) until AI capex clarity improves
defensive_moves.rationale: Markets are punishing companies that raise capex without clear revenue payoff. Meta's $125-145B AI spending forecast caused a plunge. Microsoft's $190B capex also weighed on stock. Wait for better entry points.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Hedge against oil price spikes with put options on consumer discretionary ETFs
defensive_moves.rationale: Gas prices at 4-year high pressure consumer spending. Airlines, restaurants, and retailers are vulnerable. Use puts on XLY or specific stocks like Spirit Airlines, Ford.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Increase cash allocation and reduce leverage
defensive_moves.rationale: Geopolitical risks, central bank uncertainty, and AI capex bubble create high volatility. Cash provides optionality to buy dips. Reduce margin and speculative positions.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Buy gold and precious metals as geopolitical hedge
defensive_moves.rationale: Gold rebounded as yen intervention weakened dollar. Geopolitical tensions and inflation support gold. Copper and silver miner Lumina IPO teeters, but precious metals remain attractive.

defensive_moves.recommendation: Avoid high-yield bonds and favor investment-grade credit
defensive_moves.rationale: Air Baltic restructuring fears and European junk issuers switching to fixed rates signal credit stress. Higher rates and potential recession increase default risk. Stick with IG bonds.

market_outlook.short_term: 1-3 month outlook: Markets will remain volatile due to geopolitical tensions (Iran, Taiwan), central bank hawkishness, and AI capex uncertainty. Energy and defense sectors likely to outperform. Tech may continue to sell off as investors digest mega-cap spending plans. Inflation data and oil prices will be key drivers. Dow may continue to outperform Nasdaq. Expect range-bound trading with downside risks.
market_outlook.long_term: 6-12 month outlook: If Iran war de-escalates and oil prices ease, markets could rally strongly. AI capex may eventually pay off, driving tech recovery. Central banks may cut rates in 2027 if inflation subsides. Infrastructure and defense spending remain secular tailwinds. Emerging markets could benefit from supply chain shifts. Overall, cautious optimism with a bias toward value and defensive sectors.
key_catalysts: Iran war de-escalation or escalation (Hormuz blockade resolution)
Fed, ECB, BOE rate decisions and forward guidance
Oil price trajectory (sustained above $100 or pullback)
Big Tech earnings and AI capex ROI clarity
US GDP and inflation data (PCE, CPI)
UAE OPEC exit impact on oil supply
US election cycle and policy uncertainty
Corporate earnings season (especially consumer and industrial)
Yen intervention and global currency dynamics
watch_list: WTI and Brent crude oil prices
Bitcoin and crypto market (MARA, AI mining)
